content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents can dynamically prioritize which raw interaction experiences get compressed and retained long-term based on the expected information gain of each memory, rather than just recency or frequency of access. This creates a self-improving memory loop where an agent’s intrinsic curiosity drives it to prune low-information memories and store high-uncertainty, learnable memories to improve future exploration.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
